focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 18

When HR came back around about the workstation, Bao's desk was already mostly cleared — he'd done it himself that morning, stacking his things into the cardboard box with the same deliberate care he brought to everything. Dani had volunteered to finish clearing it out, Nick had said he'd help, and now they were here in the mid-afternoon quiet of the evaluation floor, pulling open drawers that Bao had already been through.

The top drawer held a phone charger that wasn't Bao's. It had been there since before he started, one of those universal artifacts of office furniture that belonged to no one and would outlast everyone. Dani set it on the desk. The second drawer was empty except for a handful of paper clips and a rubber band shaped like a figure eight. The third drawer was deeper, designed for hanging files, and when Nick pulled it open he found a stack of loose pages, handwritten, that Bao had either forgotten or decided didn't belong to him anymore.

Nick picked up the top sheet. Bao's handwriting was small and precise — a graphic designer's lettering, each character formed with the same attention he'd given to his evaluations. The note was about a training image, or had started that way. At the top he'd written the batch number and a description: *Garden scene, residential, midday sun, generated.* Below that, where the rubric asked for a rating and perhaps a brief comment, Bao had written three dense paragraphs about the quality of green in the foliage — how the model had defaulted to a single hue across every leaf when even a backyard garden in July held fifteen or twenty distinguishable greens, and how the shadow beneath the rosebush was blue-gray when it should have been a warm violet because the reflected light from the soil would tint it, and how...
open_hooks_to_carry: Bao fired — clearing his desk with quiet dignity, shaking hands with each person
Dani's position threatened — her custody lawyer's call, the employment interruption as legal ammunition
Tommy on probation for pre-existing quality deviations swept into the audit
Priya's panic attack in the bathroom — Nick hearing it through the wall

payoffs_to_land.description: Dani's custody vulnerability, seeded in Ch 5, now faces a direct threat from the job instability Nick caused
Bao's slow evaluation pace, flagged in Ch 6, makes him the easiest target in the purge — his quality is indistinguishable from deficiency in the audit's metrics
The blunt, metrics-driven investigation pattern from Ch 14 repeats at San Leandro — same corporate machinery, now grinding through Nick's friends
Priya's accumulating stress, seeded from Ch 5/7, culminates in a panic attack

prev_tail_excerpt:
...t Bay September light right on the brown grass and eucalyptus shadow. He'd sold none of them. Bao's 4 was still the high score. Tommy had given another one a 2 for stylistic coherence. People at Canopy knew he painted. What they didn't know was that Soft Error was selling damage he had made, and that some of the source images came from the same life stacked against his spine.

He clicked into the Soft Error checkout flow just to see how far the joke went. Shipping really was fifteen dollars. The subtotal for one melted face was a week's groceries if he was careful, two parking tickets if he was not, more money than he'd made from some magazine spots back when he still took work like that. He closed the page before it asked for his address. Then he opened it again when the number on "Mandible Study" dropped by one.

He did the math without meaning to. Seven prints had gone across the site at two hundred fifty each. That was seventeen hundred fifty dollars before shipping, before whatever language they used to justify the markup, before anyone in Brooklyn talked about materiality or failure or the body under late capitalism. It was more than one Oakland-hills canvas. It was more than enough to make a month of PG&E, groceries, and gas stop feeling like a problem for a minute. Damage he had produced in order to hurt the company had become someone else's inventory and someone else's rent.

Nick started laughing.

It came out in short bursts at first, then in one hard continuous sound that bent him over his knees and made the canvases knock softly against the wall behind him. When it finally stopped, his ribs hurt, his eyes were wet, a BART train was rattling south through the dark, and the counter for edition three of "Mandible Study" had dropped from five remaining to four.
secondary_character_beats: Each person's suffering must connect to their independent pressure established earlier: Bao's firing hits his wife's care; Dani's job threat hits custody; Tommy's probation hits his loan payments; Priya's breakdown echoes the one that ended her teaching career
Nick helping carry Bao's box to his car — the specific physical act of assisting in the consequence of his own crime
state_deltas_required: The internal investigation reaches Nick's coworkers — every person he cares about pays for what he did
The human cost of the sabotage lands on the people Nick was supposed to be protecting; his moral position becomes indefensible by any logic including his own
